name: Jumbo Lump Crabcakes, Cucumber Carrot Salad A
recipeIngredient: 3/4 cup Chopped peppers (red, green, and yellow)
1 1/2 tbsp Each of fresh dill, chives, and parsley (cleaned, stems removed)
recipeInstructions:
1. Pre-heat the oven to 350 degrees.
2. In a saucepan over medium heat, combine oil, onions, and peppers. Simmer until vegetables are tender, about 7 minutes.
3. In a large bowl, whisk together egg yolks, lime juice, mustard, Tabasco, fish sauce.
4. Remove the oil and vegetables from the heat. Slowly pour into the yolk mixture, whisking constantly.
5. Pour with small splashes, incorporating the oil completely before adding more. The heat of the oil will cook the yolks as you go, resulting in a hollandaise-like sauce.
6. Return the saucepan to the heat for 30 seconds, whisking constantly to ensure the sauce is fully incorporated. Set aside over ice and whisk until chilled.
7. Make the Chipotle Sauce: Combine chipotle peppers with adobo sauce, garlic, shallots, cilantro, rice vinegar, lemon juice in a blender or food processor. Process all ingredients except soy oil. Add oil slowly until emulsified. Taste and adjust seasoning as needed. Reserve.
8. Prepare crab cakes: Pick over crab meat to remove any shell pieces. Gently toss with herbs. Add two tablespoons of the chilled sauce base and gently combine. Bind mixture with enough bread crumbs, checking that it is moist. Add more sauce or breadcrumbs if necessary. Season with white pepper.
9. Form crab into small cakes about 2 inches in diameter. In a separate bowl, beat eggs. Coat each cake with egg, then Japanese bread crumbs.
10. Heat vegetable oil in a large sauté pan over medium heat. Brown crab cakes on both sides and transfer to a platter.
11. Place crab cakes in the pre-heated oven for 2-3 minutes to heat through.
12. Make Nuoc Cham Sauce: Combine garlic, serrano chiles, sugar, rice vinegar, lemon juice, lime juice, fish sauce, soy oil, red onion slices, carrots, turnip matchsticks, and cucumber in a bowl. Blend salad ingredients and toss with half cup of Nuoc Cham Sauce.
13. To Serve: Arrange salad at one end and two crabcakes at the other. Drizzle Chipotle sauce in a zig-zag pattern between them.
Wine Suggestion: Sauvignon Blanc
Yield: 12 crabcakes, or 6 first course servings
